releases.summary: Add reusable BYOK and environment alias UI helpers for integration setup.

audiences.summary: OSS consumers can render managed/BYOK credential setup blocks and monorepo-aware env alias previews from integration manifests.
Integration UI helpers preserve redaction guarantees by displaying secret references and required status without serializing raw secret values.

upgradeSteps.title: Use the reusable credential setup block
upgradeSteps.summary: Replace bespoke integration credential setup panels with the shared setup model and components when rendering managed/BYOK flows.
instructions: Pass credential manifests and optional workspace environment config to render requirements and target-specific aliases.
Pass secret references, not raw secret values, when displaying configured BYOK credentials.

evidence: Helper tests cover legacy schema fallback, explicit manifest precedence, missing BYOK secret refs, and secret redaction.
Render tests cover managed/BYOK setup actions and Next/Expo alias previews.

releases.summary: Add first-class monorepo-aware environment contracts and managed/BYOK credential setup helpers.

audiences.summary: Monorepos can declare logical environment variables once and materialize framework-specific aliases such as NEXT_PUBLIC_* and EXPO_PUBLIC_* per app target.
Integration specs can expose managed/BYOK credential manifests while runtime reports redact secret and sensitive values.
BYOK setup can be validated from shared contracts without placing raw secrets in specs, docs, reports, or generated env examples.

upgradeSteps.title: Declare logical env variables before framework aliases
upgradeSteps.summary: Use workspace environment definitions and app targets instead of duplicating public env names across apps.
instructions: Define shared logical variables under `.contractsrc.json > environment.variables`.
Add app targets for each package/framework that needs concrete aliases.
Keep secret values behind `secretRef` or environment/secret providers.

evidence: Schema tests reject sensitive and secret values exposed through public Next/Expo aliases.
Runtime tests prove alias materialization and redaction do not serialize secret values.
Template tests split root and app-target env example output.

releases.summary: Teach the Integration Hub example to model managed/BYOK credential setup and monorepo-aware env aliases.

audiences.summary: The Integration Hub example now exposes credential setup manifests, safe secret references, and app-specific Next/Expo env aliases for managed and BYOK integrations.
Maintainers get regression-covered setup fixtures that exercise public-prefix aliasing without exposing secret fields through client env names.

upgradeSteps.title: Reuse the Integration Hub credential setup fixtures
upgradeSteps.summary: Import the setup metadata when building docs, previews, or custom BYOK onboarding flows from the example package.
instructions: Keep raw provider secrets out of config values; pass secret references such as `env://POSTHOG_PERSONAL_API_KEY` instead.
Materialize public app aliases only for non-secret fields such as public origins or project keys.

evidence: Server-render coverage asserts BYOK guidance, Next/Expo public aliases, safe secret references, and absence of public aliases for secret fields.
Build output includes the new setup and credential setup preview entrypoints for bun and node targets.

releases.summary: Improve PageOutline desktop behavior with a Notion-like floating rail that keeps AppShell content centered while expanding on hover or keyboard focus.

audiences.summary: AppShell consumers get a floating desktop PageOutline that no longer reserves a right content column, while mobile and native outline behavior remains menu-contained.
Design-system maintainers can opt PageOutline into a floating variant while preserving existing rail and compact variants.

upgradeSteps.title: Use the floating PageOutline variant for wide desktop shells
upgradeSteps.summary: AppShell now uses the floating outline automatically; direct PageOutline consumers can opt in with `variant="floating"`.
instructions: Use `variant="floating"` for direct web PageOutline usage that should reduce when inactive.
Keep `variant="rail"` or `variant="compact"` where a static inline outline is still preferred.

evidence: PageOutline render coverage exercises floating, rail, compact, anchors, and active state.
AppShell render coverage confirms page content no longer reserves the fixed right outline column.
AppShell dialog coverage confirms small-screen outline rendering remains compact.
